verses.text: He departs into Judea, and great multitudes follow, and He heals them. Constant mercy. Wherever He goes, His healing follows the needy.
He departs into Judea, and great multitudes follow, and He heals them. Constant mercy. Wherever He goes, His healing follows the needy.

Asked of divorce, He returns to creation: 'they twain shall be one flesh'; 'what... God hath joined together, let not man put asunder.' Marriage from the first. God's original design binds husband and wife as one.

Moses allowed divorce 'for the hardness of your hearts,' but it was not so from the beginning. Concession, not command. God's true will is faithfulness, not putting away.

The disciples think it better not to marry; 'he that is able to receive it, let him receive it.' A special calling. Singleness for the kingdom is a gift, not a rule.

Children are brought; 'Suffer little children... to come unto me: for of such is the kingdom.' Welcomed. Christ receives the lowly and the young.

A young man asks the way to eternal life; Jesus names the commandments. The law's mirror. He sends the seeker to see himself in God's law.
A young man asks the way to eternal life; Jesus names the commandments. The law's mirror. He sends the seeker to see himself in God's law.

Claiming to keep all, he is told, 'sell that thou hast,' and 'follow me'; he goes away sorrowful, being rich. Riches refused. His wealth held him back from Christ.
Claiming to keep all, he is told, 'sell that thou hast,' and 'follow me'; he goes away sorrowful, being rich. Riches refused. His wealth held him back from Christ.
Claiming to keep all, he is told, 'sell that thou hast,' and 'follow me'; he goes away sorrowful, being rich. Riches refused. His wealth held him back from Christ.
A rich man hardly enters the kingdom; easier a camel through a needle's eye; 'with God all things are possible.' Grace alone. Only God can save where riches bind the heart.

Peter says they forsook all; they shall sit on thrones, and all who forsake for Christ receive a hundredfold; 'many that are first shall be last.' Rich reward. No sacrifice for Christ goes unrepaid.
